Risk Description: The Council’s ability to cope with a natural disaster, malicious or accidental incident affecting civilian support or disrupting Council services.
Mitigating Action: Regularly test Emergency Plan and Service Business Continuity Plans; ensure key emergency planning staff attend regular liaison meetings and training.

Risk Description: Failure of IT systems leading to loss, corruption, or inaccuracy of data, resulting in service disruption, security breaches, and potential financial and reputational damage.
Mitigating Action: Data protection policy and procedure, Freedom of Information publication scheme, data retention policy, information breach response plan, and business continuity plans. Actions include effective system auditing, secure off-site data backup, regular penetration testing, and disaster recovery testing.

Risk Description: Breach of ICT security causes loss of service due to major IT hardware failure or electronic attack, leading to service disruption, security breaches, financial penalties, and reputational risk.
Mitigating Action: • Anti-virus software, geographically distributed servers, tested disaster recovery plan, back-ups stored off site, secondary power supply, revised security policies, and critical services’ business continuity plans include manual operation. • Effective auditing of systems and data, data backed-up securely off-site, and regular penetration testing.

Risk Description: Political changes in national priorities may result in immediate changes that require additional resource to achieve and fail to reflect priorities determined by consultation.
Mitigating Action:
• Financial & workforce planning, monitoring by CMT and Cabinet reports, clear corporate planning and performance monitoring, effective service & financial planning, responding to national consultation, and membership of LGA.
• Understanding and acting on intelligence from LGA, CIPFA, and other sources, ensuring resources are identified and implemented without delay, constant monitoring, horizon scanning, and joint/collaborative working.

Risk Description: Failure of contractors and suppliers working on the Council’s behalf to deliver services or meet agreed performance objectives, leading to additional costs or failed objectives.
Mitigating Action: Procurement processes include financial checks and robust contracts with SLAs. Regular contract monitoring, trained staff, and business continuity plans are in place to manage performance and mitigate failure.

Risk Description: Major health and safety incident at Council leads to costs for inquiry, disruption to service and possible prosecution.
Mitigating Action: Ensure health and safety is standard agenda on all team meetings, maintain equipment inventory and inspections, review risk assessments and action plans, and capture and assess near misses for learning points.

Risk Description: Service provision and performance affected by organisational change, industrial action, and/or staff sickness resulting in complaints, poor performance, and possible further costs.
Mitigating Action: Business continuity plans for each service, workforce planning including succession planning, a comprehensive health surveillance programme, and a health and wellbeing programme with occupational health support and an Employee Assistance Programme.

Risk Description: Legislative changes requiring significant organizational alterations, including GDPR breach risk and ICO sanctions.
Mitigating Action: Monitoring Officer and Legal/CMT/Mgt Team conduct horizon scanning; staff are trained, procedures updated, and the Council responds to consultations to influence legislative development. A dedicated GDPR Officer and service leads ensure compliance through training and an Information Asset Register.

Risk Description: Brexit uncertainty during transition period, followed by potential legislative, funding and policy changes after UK leaves EU may adversely affect the Council and its ability to provide services.
Mitigating Action: Horizon scanning by Legal Services, CMT, and Heads of Service, alongside financial and workforce planning, membership in professional bodies, and a management of change approach. Detailed project plans are in place to manage the implementation of changes.

Using intelligence to model and plan for future changes, regular monitoring and reporting, workforce planning, national lobbying, sharing the Efficiency Plan with the Government, and pursuing shared services and partnerships.
Risk Description: Funding changes make Council unsustainable due to economic changes, imposed savings requirements, changes to local government funding systems, and uncertainties of pilot pension fund. Financial management of NNDR and CTS leads to changes in income/spending.

Mitigating Action: Increase staff vigilance and fraud awareness training for managers. Raise profile internally and externally for successful prosecutions.
Risk Description: Fraud and error committed against the Council, including potential for fraud, corruption, malpractice, or error by internal or external threats, leading to financial loss, reputational harm, and additional costs.
Failure of Governance in major partners or in the Council as a result of partnership working, leading to unachieved priorities and poor performance by partner agencies.
Mitigating Action: Assurance that governance models are correctly followed and in the Council’s interests, supported by internal audit, member support, and ensuring robust business continuity, GDPR, and ICT governance processes across all partnerships.

Heightened analysis of budgets and services by CMT, implementation of Service Transformation and Procurement Strategy, and robust control of the corporate Transformation Plan with regular progress reports to Members.
Risk Description: Failure to achieve required savings targets, resulting in greater than budgeted costs and potential risk of Council not being able to set a balanced budget.
Capital funding strategy failure leading to financial risks of capital funding shortfalls and potential marginal deficit in capital program if future funding is not realised.
Mitigating Action: Asset Management Plan and disposal strategy, regular Cabinet and CMT monitoring of capital receipts and programme, pursuit of additional funding, and forward planning with horizon scanning.
• CSE Action Plan, staff survey, public consultations on key issues, 3cs refresher training, team meetings, and regular updates from the Chief Executive to all staff.
Risk Description: Poor communication with stakeholders and staff leads to poorly informed direction of resources, lack of support for change, reputational damage, increased staff turnover, and sickness absence.

Mitigating Action: Robust oversight and governance arrangements, expert professional advice, robust budget management, and thorough project management and business cases process.
Risk Description: Commercial uncertainties associated with decisions taken as part of the Council’s Commercial and Investment Strategy.

Mitigating Action:
• Policy for maximum investment/borrowing levels limits liability, with credit ratings, financial management, reserves, insurance, and strategies in place.
• Effective Treasury Management strategy and robust auditing of processes and policies.
Risk Description: Failure of external investment institutions affecting availability of funds or return on investment, reducing cash flow and resource availability.
Insufficient staff to provide Council services; insufficient leadership and/or management capacity to deliver Council priorities.
Mitigating Action:
• Ensure all services have effective Workforce plans incorporated into Service Plans, which ensure all work is prioritised.
• Effective succession planning.
• Effective use of project management approaches/ principles when delivering priorities/ strategies.

Risk Description: Lack of access to Council premises prevents services being delivered, causing disruption of service provision.
Mitigating Action: Regularly test Emergency Plan and service Business Continuity Plans; ensure key emergency planning staff attend liaison meetings and training; provide 'drop down' facilities for staff.
Robust project management and effective risk registers for projects.
Risk Description: Over-run of major Council projects in time or cost. Failure to manage projects effectively leads to overruns and failure to achieve project aims, causing reputational damage.
